t_ch: 「持明龙尊沉吟道：『你我二人齐心协力，应能与这绝灭大君势均力敌，可若要确保胜利，则还需要一把神兵利器。』」
t_en: "The high elder of the Vidyadhara sternly replied: 'With our forces combined, we should be evenly matched against the Lord Ravager. But a divine weapon would guarantee victory.'"

t_ch: 「书中暗表，这{TEXTJOIN#59}保留了不少前世记忆，其中就有锻造这上古神兵的秘法。」
t_en: "The book implies that {TEXTJOIN#59} retains memories from previous reincarnations, including the secrets of forging such an ancient ultimate weapon."

t_ch: 「这折书便是『斩妖邪义侠锻神兵，镇玄根仙舟板荡平』！」
t_en: "This next scene is called: Forging Weapon, Purging Demon!"

t_ch: 怎么样？您二位准备好了吗？咱们再一起构思一下，怎么把这个故事给写圆满吧。
t_en: What do you think? Are you both ready? Let's work together to bring this story to a nice conclusion.

t_ch: 准备好了，开始吧。
t_en: I'm ready. Let's begin.

t_ch: 稍微等一下。
t_en: Hang on a sec.

t_ch: 无妨，不过还是稍微抓紧点时间把。我晚些时候还要去一个晚会走穴。
t_en: It's okay, but let's hurry up a bit. I've got a show at a party tonight.

t_ch: 怎么样？您二位准备好了吗？咱们再一起构思一下，怎么把这个故事给写圆满吧。
t_en: What do you think? Are you both ready? Let's work together to bring this story to a nice conclusion.

t_ch: 准备好了，开始吧。
t_en: I'm ready. Let's begin.

t_ch: 稍微等一下。
t_en: Hang on a sec.

t_ch: 无妨，不过还是稍微抓紧点时间把。我晚些时候还要去一个堂会走穴。
t_en: It's okay, but let's hurry up a bit. I've got a show at a house party tonight.

t_ch: 这幻胧劫夺建木力量，生就一副不死不灭的躯壳，百杀不死，便是身为龙尊的我也无可奈何。
t_en: Phantylia seized the Ambrosial Arbor's power and created an invincible body. It's unkillable — even I, as high elder, remain powerless against it.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，若真欲为仙舟诛除此獠，唯有铸成传古神兵，能覆灭一切生机的「碎星绝尘剑+<unbreak>12</unbreak>」。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, if you really want to help purge this beast from the Xianzhou, we'll have to forge a divine weapon: The life-destroying "Star-Shattering Blade of Truth +<unbreak>12</unbreak>".

t_ch: 战斗力升级有些夸张了…
t_en: That power level increase sounds a little extreme...

t_ch: 名字倒是挺唬人的。
t_en: That name sounds pretty formidable.

t_ch: 「+<unbreak>12</unbreak>」是什么情况？
t_en: What's up with the "+<unbreak>12</unbreak>"?

t_ch: 大概就是为了表达很厉害的程度。
t_en: Probably just to show off how powerful it is.

t_ch: 我就按着小衍剧本念的啊，他的第一稿向来都很粗疏草率，不碍事。
t_en: I'll just follow Xiyan's script. His first drafts are usually pretty sloppy. It doesn't matter.

t_ch: 要锻造出这把足以与幻胧匹敌的碎星绝尘剑，需要两个部件。
t_en: To stand any hope of standing toe-to-toe against Phantylia, there's going to be two parts needed to forge this Star-Shattering Blade of Truth.

t_ch: 其一，是建木制成的柄材。其二，则是以帝弓光矢的余烬「天火熔金」打造的剑身。
t_en: One is a hilt forged from the wood of the Ambrosial Arbor. The other is a blade forged from the embers of Reignbow's Lux Arrow, known as the Ignis Aureliae.

t_ch: 现在我们在这迴星港，就是要找到可以制作刀柄的材料。
t_en: We are at Stargazer Navalia to search for materials to craft a sword hilt.

t_ch: 用建木做柄挺危险的吧？
t_en: Won't it be dangerous crafting a hilt from the Ambrosial Arbor's wood?

t_ch: 话是没错。况且眼下建木受幻胧劫持，咱们就是想找也无处可寻啊。不过……
t_en: It will be. Moreover, with the Ambrosial Arbor hijacked by Phantylia, we just can't go looking even if we want to. But...

t_ch: 迴星港哪儿来的建木？
t_en: Where can I find Arbor wood at Stargazer Navalia?

t_ch: 你可能有所不知，仙舟上用于培育星槎的那些种子，本质上与建木同根同源。用它来制作刀柄，虽比不上真正的建木，但打败区区一个绝灭大君，还是够用的。
t_en: You might not be aware that the seeds used by the Xianzhou to grow starskiffs are of the same origin as the Arbor. Using such wood to make sword hilts won't be the same as using wood straight off the Arbor, but it's more than enough to defeat one mere Lord Ravager.

t_ch: 现在嘛…我们需要一些星槎生长促进剂，来促进星槎种子的生长，为我们提供足够分量的柄材。
t_en: Now then... We need some starskiff growth boosters to stimulate these seeds — and give us enough material to make a hilt.

t_ch: 这里本来就是培育星槎的地方，附近应该有不少，我们就地取材，在附近找找看吧。
t_en: It's always been a starskiff nursery here, and there should be plenty nearby. Let's take a look around and see if we can use what we've got at hand.

t_ch: 这里本来就是培育星槎的地方，附近应该有不少，我们就地取材，在附近找找看吧。
t_en: It's always been a starskiff nursery here, and there should be plenty nearby. Let's take a look around and see if we can use what we've got at hand.

t_ch: 这应该就是{TEXTJOIN#59}要找的生长促进剂了。拿回去找他吧。
t_en: This should be the growth booster {TEXTJOIN#59} was looking for. Let's take it back to him.

t_ch: 你找到生长促进剂了？我尝尝品质好不好……
t_en: Did you find the growth booster? Let me taste it and see if it's any good...

t_ch: 这玩意儿有尝尝的吗！
t_en: Is it safe to taste this stuff?

t_ch: 当心促进你舌头的生长…
t_en: Hope it doesn't stimulate any growth of your tongue...

t_ch: 会不会让嘴变大？
t_en: Won't your mouth grow bigger?

t_ch: 前调有股幽幽异香…嗯，品质很高！接下来，只要找到合适的星槎种子，就能培育出合适的柄材了。
t_en: The top note has a faint whiff of alien perfume... Mmm, this is of high quality! Next, we just need a suitable starskiff seed, and we'll be able to cultivate a good sword hilt.

t_ch: 接下来，该选一颗星槎种子培育剑柄良材了。
t_en: Next, it's time to choose a starskiff seed from which to cultivate some good material for the sword hilt.

t_ch: 注入催长剂，龙尊随即存想默念，运起云吟奇术。
t_en: After injecting the growth booster, the high elder sat in meditation as he performed his cloudhymn.

t_ch: 器皿里的种子突然暴长开来，一时间枝蔓纷乱，有如乱麻！
t_en: The seed grew explosively, sprouting wild tendrils in seconds like a thick tangle of roots!

t_ch: 呃…怎么就变成这个样子了！看来是找错种子了。
t_en: Huh... How did that happen!? I guess that was the wrong seed.

t_ch: 注入催长剂，龙尊随即存想默念，运起云吟奇术。
t_en: After injecting the growth booster, the high elder sat in meditation as he performed his cloudhymn.

t_ch: 器皿中的种子突然暴长开来，迅速形成一艘船的骨架。
t_en: The seed grew explosively, sprouting rapidly into the hull of a ship.

t_ch: 竟然立刻长成了一艘星槎…看来催长实验失败了。我总不能握着一艘星槎和人打架。
t_en: It grew straight into a starskiff... Our growth-boosting experiment failed. No one can wield a starskiff in combat.

t_ch: 注入催长剂，龙尊随即存想默念，运起云吟奇术。
t_en: After injecting the growth booster, the high elder sat in meditation as he performed his cloudhymn.

t_ch: 器皿中的种子缓缓长开，无数细枝又千丝万缕地汇成一线，如有巧手将它织成剑柄的形状。
t_en: The seed in the vessel gradually sprout and countless twigs converged into one, as if an invisible hand was weaving it into the shape of a hilt.

t_ch: 很好！只有建木制成的柄材，才能承受住「天火熔金」中所存的帝弓神力，使得它不至于反噬持有者。
t_en: Wonderful! Only a hilt made from the Ambrosial Arbor can hope to withstand the Reignbow's residual power in the Ignis Aureliae, so it would not harm its wielder.

t_ch: 接下来，我们去丹鼎司吧。如果前世的记忆准确，那里收存着铸成神兵剑体的「天火熔金」。
t_en: Now let's head over to the Alchemy Commission. If my memories from the previous life are correct, the Ignis Aureliae, which can cast the divine weapon, is stored over there.

t_ch: 这碎星绝尘剑，乃是饮月之乱时丹枫所用的神器。
t_en: This Star-Shattering Blade of Truth is a divine artifact once wielded by Dan Feng during the Sedition of Imbibitor Lunae.

t_ch: 所谓「邦之利器，不可以示人」，他在系狱之前，已将「天火熔金」秘藏了起来，以防宵小觊觎。
t_en: It is said that "A Sword Is Mightier When Hidden." He, therefore, hid the Ignis Aureliae away, from potential thieves before he was imprisoned.

t_ch: 所幸我依然留存着前世回忆，依稀记得在丹鼎司某处，丹枫将藏有熔金的地点绘成图示，留在司内某处。也许，找到这张图示，宝藏自然现世。
t_en: Fortunately, I still retain memories from my previous lives, and can dimly recall that Dan Feng has hidden a map somewhere deep in the Alchemy Commission that shows where the Ignis Aureliae is. Perhaps we can find this, and the treasure will be practically revealed to us.

t_ch: 又是喜闻乐见的寻宝环节？
t_en: Let me guess, it's treasure huntin' time, innit?

t_ch: 同样的套路是否太多了？
t_en: Isn't fetch quest a bit overused?

t_ch: 这有什么！星际和平娱乐还一口气拍过六十多部以武装学士艾伦•琼斯为原型的《夺宝奇谋》系列电影。寻宝套路，老少咸宜啊！
t_en: So what? Interastral Peace Entertainment has shot over sixty films in the Allen Jones treasure-hunting franchise. Treasure hunts are family-friendly fun!

t_ch: 瞧见了没，这就是标示熔金所在之处的地图！
t_en: See, here's the map showing where the Ignis Aureliae is!

t_ch: 只消找到其上标出的位置，距离神器现世的那一刻就不远了。
t_en: Just track down the location shown and you'll be moments away from creating the divine weapon.

t_ch: 千回百转，{NICKNAME}没想到自己会再次回到起点——丹鼎司中所谓「太真丹室」的巨大炉鼎之下。
t_en: After all these twists and turns, {NICKNAME} never thought things would end up right back at the start: Under the almighty Aureate Elixir Furnace in the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 太真二字，乃黄金之古称。丹枫将宝藏置于此处，正如藏木于林，虽答案近在咫尺，数百年来却并无一人勘破。
t_en: The word "Aureate" pertains to the word "gold." Dan Feng hiding his treasure here is like hiding a tree in a forest — so close, and yet undiscovered for several hundred years.

t_ch: 这叙事扣子，你们觉得我回收的怎么样？
t_en: How do you like my little callback for this mini cliffhanger?

t_ch: （无语凝噎）
t_en: (Stares speechlessly)

t_ch: 这边的丹士就没一个发觉吗？
t_en: Didn't any of the alchemists around here ever notice it?

t_ch: 我对你的编剧才能感到绝望…
t_en: There's no saving grace for your screenwriting skills...

t_ch: 口下留情啊，石韵老师在一旁，别让他看我笑话啊。
t_en: Please go easy on me and don't make Shiyun laugh at me.

t_ch: 说时迟，那时快，正当{NICKNAME}与{TEXTJOIN#59}欲深入炉鼎，起出熔金时，一众身影拦住去路。
t_en: No sooner said than done, just as {NICKNAME} and {TEXTJOIN#59} were about to dive into the elixir cauldron and unearth the Ignis Aureliae, a group of figures blocked their path.

t_ch: 呵，该打架了是吧？
t_en: O-ho! Looking for a fight, are we?

t_ch: 宝藏当前，必有恶龙啊…
t_en: Some good old treasure guarding villainy, huh?

t_ch: 奉幻胧大人之命，今日誓言要将尔等斩于胯下！
t_en: Under the orders of Master Phantylia, I hereby vow to cut you all down!

t_ch: 好个幻胧，竟早已预感到了{NICKNAME}一行的异动，打算以宝藏为饵，将无名客们一网成擒！
t_en: How cunning! Realizing {NICKNAME} and the others' unusual actions, Phantylia was planning on using the treasure as bait to lure the Nameless into its trap!

t_ch: 这妖物口出狂言，且来试一试我罗浮持明的神威！
t_en: This devil is delusional! Taste the might of the Luofu Vidyadhara!

t_ch: 幻胧自以为派出手下精锐便能得计。却不知今日的{NICKNAME}有龙尊助阵，远胜往昔。反物质军团不消几合便大败亏输，神形俱灭。
t_en: Phantylia assumed dispatching crack troops would be enough to win. Little did it know that today, {NICKNAME} had the high elder's support and was far stronger than before. The Antimatter Legion was routed before long, and utterly annihilated without a trace.

t_ch: 龙尊一拂衣袖，托手如云，似同眼前的巨大丹炉交相感应。只见丹炉中奇光迸现（此处请幻戏剧组加强特效），一团流动的金辉如水般在空中攒聚成形——
t_en: With a flick of his sleeve, the high elder elegantly moved his hands like billowing clouds, as if communicating telepathically with the huge elixir crucible before him. A strange light burst forth from the crucible (by the way, this is where the VFX artists can add some Immersia special effects), and a lump of molten brilliance coalesced like condensation forming in the air —

t_ch: 这正是铸成碎星绝尘剑的剑材，「天火熔金」是也！
t_en: This is the Ignis Aureliae that would cast the Star-Shattering Blade of Truth!

t_ch: 天金既得，接下来便是将它们铸合一体的过程了。要完成这项艰巨工作，非得依靠工造司中造化洪炉才能办得到。
t_en: With the Ignis Aureliae in hand, the next step was to forge them into one instrument. This arduous task will need a trip to the Artisanship Commission to borrow their Creation Furnace.

t_ch: 那咱们这就走呗。
t_en: Then let's hit the road.

t_ch: 你让我喘口气缓缓…
t_en: Let me catch my breath...

t_ch: 喘够了没？我接下来还有活儿要接呢，快着点。
t_en: Rested up enough? I've got work to do later, so hurry up.

t_ch: 那咱们这就走呗。
t_en: Then let's hit the road.

t_ch: 你让我喘口气缓缓…
t_en: Let me catch my breath...

t_ch: 「二位英杰杀入鳞渊深处，只见一道黑影盘踞于建木玄根，正是那欲倾覆仙舟的万恶罪魁——幻胧！」
t_en: "The two heroes plunged deep into Scalegorge Waterscape, only to see a black shadow enshrouding the Ambrosial Arbor. It was none other than the root of all evil, the one trying to overthrow the Xianzhou - Phantylia!"

t_ch: 「此时此刻，幻胧自建木中汲取丰饶神力已到了紧要关头，这位绝灭大君轻蔑一哂：不知死活的蝼蚁们，纳命来！」
t_en: "Right here, right now, was the critical moment for Phantylia to absorb Abundance's powers from the Ambrosial Arbor. The Lord Ravager sneered: Reckless ants, accept your fate!"

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}举起神兵，夷然不惧，应道——」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} raised the divine weapon fearlessly, and dropped this line —"

t_ch: 这一击，贯穿星辰？
t_en: Will this blow pierce through the stars?

t_ch: 我一定会回来的！
t_en: Hasta la vista, bay-bee!

t_ch: 啊？到我说台词了？
t_en: ...Ah? What's my line again?

t_ch: 啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊！
t_en: Aaaaaaaaaargh!!

t_ch: 真是一场艰苦的旅程啊，{NICKNAME}。眼下万事齐备，可以开始铸造那碎星绝尘剑了…
t_en: It's been a rough journey, {NICKNAME}. But now that everything's in hand, we can start with the business of forging this Star-Shattering Blade of Truth...

t_ch: 要铸造出这把神兵，有三者不可或缺，天材地宝不过其一。{NICKNAME}，你可知还有其他哪两样不可缺少？
t_en: There are three conditions we need to meet in order to forge this divine weapon, and the sacred materials we have collected so far only count as one. {NICKNAME}, do you know what the other two are?

t_ch: 随心所欲的设定…
t_en: Some random worldbuilding rules you just made up...

t_ch: 胡编乱造的作者…
t_en: Some screenwriting nonsense...

t_ch: 小衍，你找的这{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}到底行不行啊？一点都不敬业。
t_en: Xiyan, is this {F#girl}{M#boy} going to be okay on set? Totally unprofessional.

t_ch: 您老息怒，这位贵客打救罗浮，自是不同于凡庸之辈，身上是带些个傲气的。
t_en: It's ok, my old friend. This esteemed guest has rescued the Luofu, and naturally, {F#she}{M#he} is no ordinary layperson. After all, all heroes have a bit of an ego.

t_ch: 行吧，咱们接着说，铸成神兵所需的其余两大要素，自然就是咱们身处的这工造司了。工欲善其事，必先利其器。若没有此处的铸造设备和无根纯水，神器难成！
t_en: Alright, let's continue. The two other conditions needed to create the divine weapon are right here in our Artisanship Commission. As they say, you need the right tools for the job. Without the smelting equipment and Bona Aqua here, creating a divine weapon would be impossible!

t_ch: 至于最后一样，则是铸造神剑的工序。幸亏我依然存有前生回忆……
t_en: As for the last condition, it's about how we forge the weapon. Fortunately, I still have memories from my previous life to serve us...

t_ch: 什么都记得，你不就是丹枫？
t_en: If you remember everything, doesn't that mean you're basically Dan Feng?

t_ch: 关于「我是谁」这样深奥的哲学问题，咱先按下不表。
t_en: Let's put aside for the time being the profound philosophical concept of "Who Am I?"

t_ch: 这故事还有什么是不可以的？
t_en: Is there anything that can't happen in this story?

t_ch: 这神兵的铸造过程是这样的，你且听我道来——
t_en: The forging process for the divine weapon is like this. Just listen to me —

t_ch: 「天降无根水，烈火复熔金。坎离方既济，木德始化育。玄根沐天霖，顽铁生剑心。锋芒摧星垓，玉界绝尘埃。」
t_en: "Heaven Rains Aqua Vitae, Flames Forge Veritas Aureliae. Aether Tilts Askew, Arbor Sprouts Anew. Root Bathes in Empyreal Dew, Iron Grows With Ephemeral Hue. Blade Shatters Starscape, Jade Eclipses Agroshade."

t_ch: 你，学会了吗？
t_en: Did you get all that?

t_ch: 您要不给解释解释？
t_en: How about you give an explanation?

t_ch: 也就是说先注入<color=#dbc291ff>「无根纯水」</color>，再加入<color=#dbc291ff>「天火熔金」</color>，等到它将纯水全部蒸发，就加入<color=#dbc291ff>「冷却液」</color>。完全冷却之后，将<color=#dbc291ff>「建木柄材」</color>放入，再加入<color=#dbc291ff>「无根纯水」</color>，剑材与剑柄连为一体，宛如天成。
t_en: What that means is, first add the <color=#dbc291ff>Bona Aqua</color>, then add the <color=#dbc291ff>Ignis Aureliae</color>. When the Bona Aqua has completely evaporated, add <color=#dbc291ff>coolant</color>. After it has completely cooled, put in the <color=#dbc291ff>hilt-wood from the Arbor</color>, then add more <color=#dbc291ff>Bona Aqua</color> to fuse the hilt with the metal into a single instrument, as though they have always been one.

t_ch: 这歌诀它不押韵…
t_en: This verse doesn't rhyme that perfectly...

t_ch: 嘿，你这吹毛求疵…这本子又不是我写的，格律押韵什么的跟作者探讨去。
t_en: Hey, that's pretty picky... I didn't write this part anyway, so take up any criticism about the poetry with the author.

t_ch: 我听懂了…
t_en: I understand...

t_ch: 记住了吗？接下来，你要前往工造司深处，以此口诀铸炼宝剑。
t_en: Got it? Next, go to the depths of the Artisanship Commission and use this formula to forge the weapon.

t_ch: 唉！虽然我也想亲自让神兵重新现世，但奈何我{TEXTJOIN#59}才刚出生，还身材矮小…如果没有其他问题的话，就请你开始锻造吧。
t_en: Well! Even though I'd like to personally bear witness to the reawakening of this divine weapon, I — {TEXTJOIN#59} — have only just been reborn, so I'm still not very tall... If there are no further questions, please start with the forging yourself.

t_ch: 那我去锻造了…
t_en: I'll go forge it then...

t_ch: 锻造工序是什么来着？
t_en: What's the forging process again?

t_ch: 也就是说先注入<color=#dbc291ff>「无根纯水」</color>，再加入<color=#dbc291ff>「天火熔金」</color>，等到它将纯水全部蒸发，就加入<color=#dbc291ff>「冷却液」</color>。完全冷却之后，将<color=#dbc291ff>「建木柄材」</color>放入，再加入<color=#dbc291ff>「无根纯水」</color>，剑材与剑柄连为一体，宛如天成。
t_en: What that means is, first add the <color=#dbc291ff>Bona Aqua</color>, then add the <color=#dbc291ff>Ignis Aureliae</color>. When the Bona Aqua has completely evaporated, add <color=#dbc291ff>coolant</color>. After it has completely cooled, put in the <color=#dbc291ff>hilt-wood from the Arbor</color>, then add more <color=#dbc291ff>Bona Aqua</color> to fuse the hilt with the metal into a single instrument, as though they have always been one.

t_ch: 这规矩非常严谨，马虎不得，你可别弄错了。
t_en: This formula is really strict. Don't mess it up or make any mistakes.

t_ch: 好，准备开始锻造神兵利器了…第一步，应该先怎么着来着？
t_en: Alright. I'm ready to start forging the divine weapon... Step one... What am I doing again?

t_ch: 放入「建木柄材」
t_en: Put in the hilt-wood from the Arbor

t_ch: 放入「无根纯水」
t_en: Put in the Bona Aqua

t_ch: 放入「天火熔金」
t_en: Put in the Ignis Aureliae

t_ch: 放入「冷却液」
t_en: Put in the coolant

t_ch: 好的，然后下一步应该放什么来着……
t_en: Alright. What's the next step...

t_ch: 放入「建木柄材」
t_en: Put in the hilt-wood from the Arbor

t_ch: 放入「无根纯水」
t_en: Put in the Bona Aqua

t_ch: 放入「天火熔金」
t_en: Put in the Ignis Aureliae

t_ch: 放入「冷却液」
t_en: Put in the coolant

t_ch: 第三步，应该是放入这个东西吧……
t_en: Step three now. I should put this in, right...?

t_ch: 放入「建木柄材」
t_en: Put in the hilt-wood from the Arbor

t_ch: 放入「无根纯水」
t_en: Put in the Bona Aqua

t_ch: 放入「天火熔金」
t_en: Put in the Ignis Aureliae

t_ch: 放入「冷却液」
t_en: Put in the coolant

t_ch: 武器差不多成型了…下一步该装柄材了。该怎么装来着？
t_en: The weapon's almost finished... The next step is installing the hilt. How does that get done again?

t_ch: 先放入柄材，再加入无根纯水。
t_en: Put the hilt-wood in first, and then the Bona Aqua.

t_ch: 先加入无根纯水，再放入柄材。
t_en: Put the Bona Aqua in first, and then the hilt-wood.

t_ch: 将柄材雕刻好后安装上去。
t_en: Sculpt the hilt-wood and then install it.

t_ch: 呼～终于锻造好了！工序应该没出错吧？
t_en: Phew~ Finally forged! I did the process correctly, right?

t_ch: 好像出错了，再锻造一遍吧…
t_en: I think I've messed up. Let's try that again...

t_ch: 就这样吧！
t_en: So be it!

t_ch: 碎星绝尘剑似乎是它之前的名字，这把神兵随着龙尊沦为罪囚而蒙尘受辱。而今得你妙手重铸，不如重新为它起个名字吧？
t_en: Star-Shattering Blade of Truth is this weapon's old name, and it's been dragged through the mud since the fall of the high elder. Now that you've re-cast it with skill and precision, why not give it a new name too?

t_ch: 维持原名吧！
t_en: Keep the old name!

t_ch: 「碎星绝尘剑」，剑出星碎，万古绝尘！端的是好名字！
t_en: Star-Shattering Blade of Truth: Slash at the stars, and leave foes shattered. A grandiose and elaborate name!

t_ch: 大宝剑！
t_en: Big Sword!

t_ch: 言简意赅，不落窠臼！大，宝，剑！端的是好名字！
t_en: Concise, but unconventional! Big, sword... Truly a splendid name!

t_ch: 风灵月影剑！
t_en: Reaper of Nexus Modder!

t_ch: 我就知道{NICKNAME}也是满腹才学啊，此剑如风有灵，如月照影，正合其名「风灵月影」，端的是好名字！
t_en: Ha! I always knew that the great {NICKNAME} was a very cultured {F#young lady}{M#young man}. This blade is more than just a physical weapon — it connects, deciphers, and even modifies the rules of the world itself. An unusual but fitting name!

t_ch: 虚数内能便携式战术激光炮！
t_en: Imaginary Intra-Kinetic Portable Tactical Laser Cannon!

t_ch: 呃…虽然还想再试一次，但「天火熔金」似乎告罄了…算了！不重来了！摆烂了！
t_en: Er... Really, I'd like to try again but I think we're out of Ignis Aureliae... Never mind then! Just deal with it, okay!?

t_ch: 顺其自然吧！
t_en: Go with the flow!

t_ch: 碎星绝尘剑似乎是它之前的名字，这把神兵随着龙尊沦为罪囚而蒙尘受辱。而今得你妙手重铸，不如重新为它起个名字吧？
t_en: Star-Shattering Blade of Truth is this weapon's old name, and it's been dragged through the mud since the fall of the high elder. Now that you've re-cast it with skill and precision, why not give it a new name too?

t_ch: 维持原名吧！
t_en: Keep the old name!

t_ch: 「碎星绝尘剑」，剑出星碎，万古绝尘！端的是好名字！
t_en: Star-Shattering Blade of Truth: Slash at the stars, and leave foes shattered. A grandiose and elaborate name!

t_ch: 大宝剑！
t_en: Big Sword!

t_ch: 言简意赅，不落窠臼！大，宝，剑！端的是好名字！
t_en: Concise, but unconventional! Big, sword... Truly a splendid name!

t_ch: 风灵月影剑！
t_en: Reaper of Nexus Modder!

t_ch: 我就知道{NICKNAME}也是满腹才学啊，此剑如风有灵，如月照影，正合其名「风灵月影」，端的是好名字！
t_en: Ha! I always knew that the great {NICKNAME} was a very cultured {F#young lady}{M#young man}. This blade is more than just a physical weapon — it connects, deciphers, and even modifies the rules of the world itself. An unusual but fitting name!

t_ch: 虚数内能便携式战术激光炮！
t_en: Imaginary Intra-Kinetic Portable Tactical Laser Cannon!

t_ch: 等等，这…「激光炮」是什么东西啊？
t_en: Hang on... What's this "laser cannon" about?

t_ch: 虚数内能便携式战术激光炮！
t_en: Imaginary Intra-Kinetic Portable Tactical Laser Cannon!

t_ch: 可…可按照我这设定，这东西原本可叫做碎星绝尘剑啊！
t_en: But... But based on my screenplay, this should be called the Star-Shattering Blade of Truth!

t_ch: 谁说「碎星绝尘剑」就不能是一门激光炮了？现在市面上都是些乏善可陈的仙侠幻戏，观众早就看腻了。
t_en: Who said the Star-Shattering Blade of Truth can't actually be a laser cannon? The market is saturated with boring superhero Immersia, and the audience wants something new.

t_ch: 咱们推陈出新，在这儿给它冒出一个{TEXTJOIN#61}，给他们一点小小的科幻震撼！我说小衍，你这剧本还想不想卖咱们幻戏社了？
t_en: Out with the old and in with the new. Let's use {TEXTJOIN#61} here and give them a bit of a sci-fi shock! How about it, Xiyan — or aren't you interested in selling us this script anymore?

t_ch: 行吧，我犟不过您二位…{TEXTJOIN#61}就{TEXTJOIN#61}吧……
t_en: Fine. You two are simply too much... If {TEXTJOIN#61} is what you want, then {TEXTJOIN#61} it is...

t_ch: 「{TEXTJOIN#61}重临世间，{NICKNAME}光是握住它，就觉得自己所向披靡。」
t_en: "{TEXTJOIN#61}, reborn once more, settled in {NICKNAME}'s hand — who suddenly felt invincible."

t_ch: 「内有『九转升天丹』脱胎换骨，外有持明龙尊强援臂助，手中还有足以毁天灭地的神兵利器{TEXTJOIN#61}！」
t_en: "With the dragon oil elixir flowing internally and the Vidyadhara high elder's power protecting externally, our hero gripped onto this world-destroying divine blade, {TEXTJOIN#61}!"

t_ch: 「此时，在{NICKNAME}心中只有一个念头——消灭绝灭大君，保得仙舟万代太平。」
t_en: "At this time, there is only one thought in {NICKNAME}'s head - The Lord Ravager must be destroyed so the Xianzhou can remain in peace."

t_ch: 大君绝灭！
t_en: Lord Ravager!

t_ch: 万代太平！
t_en: Everlasting peace!

t_ch: 大结局了！
t_en: The grand finale!

t_ch: 下回分解！
t_en: Stay tuned for next time!

t_ch: 「随着幻胧的身体轰然倒下，仙舟罗浮的灾难也暂告一段落了…然而，正当{NICKNAME}与{TEXTJOIN#59}准备庆贺时，隐藏在深处的邪恶造物却伸出了魔爪！」
t_en: "As Phantylia's body crashed down, the crisis of the Xianzhou Luofu came to an end for now... However, just as {NICKNAME} and {TEXTJOIN#59} were getting ready to celebrate, an evil creature lurking deep within the depths reached out its malevolent talons!"

t_ch: 您先等一下…
t_en: Hold on a second...

t_ch: 哎呀，您别着急嘛。之前都说了很多次了嘛，战斗桥段在下分分钟就可以写完，不要急，我等一下就写。
t_en: Ah, don't worry. I've told you many times already — the battle scenes write themselves. It's fine. I'll finish them up later.

t_ch: 不是这个问题…
t_en: That's not the problem...

t_ch: 幻胧不长那个猩猩样！
t_en: Phantylia does not look like THAT!

t_ch: 嗐…这我可是完全按照您的描述设计的！背后长着植物、身形非常巨大、看起来特别凶恶……
t_en: Hey... This was based on your descriptions! Plants on the back, monstrously huge, ferocious...

t_ch: 算了，{NICKNAME}，别计较了，就当是艺术加工吧。真要长得像绝灭大君那么可怕，拍成幻戏的成本也要提高不少。
t_en: Forget it, {NICKNAME}. Just forget about that. Treat it as part of the artistic process. The budget for shooting Immersia would shoot through the roof if we really wanted to capture a ferocious and accurate likeness of the Lord Ravager.

t_ch: 对对对，你先别计较这些…等我之后再做修改。咳咳，在下还是先继续给故事收个尾啊……
t_en: Yes, that's right. Don't worry about these details... I'll revise them later. Ahem, I'll keep going and bring the story to its denouement, then...

t_ch: 「面对前所未有的强敌，开拓者与持明龙尊的命运，究竟会走向何方呢？下一折便是最终决战——『仙舟民同悲万古尘，开拓者星落长乐天』！」
t_en: "Faced with an enemy of unprecedented power, what will become of the Trailblazer and the high elder of the Vidyadhara? The next scene is our final decisive battle: Xianzhou Laments Woes of Age, Trailblazer Fails Sanctum of Exaltation!"

t_ch: 我怎么就星落了！
t_en: Wait a sec, why do I fail in this!?

t_ch: 你怎么标题剧透！
t_en: Hey, that title is a spoiler!

t_ch: 哎呀，就是一点点下集预告的小噱头罢了…在下才不舍得真给您写死呢。呼…感谢您二位的协助！在下这个台本啊，总算是写完了。
t_en: Come on, it's just a little gimmick for the next scene's preview... I'm not really going to kill you off. ...Whew, finally finished it. Thank you both for your help! This script of mine is pretty much done now.

t_ch: 那看起来没我什么事了？我先回去了啊。等你这边定稿了，记得发给幻戏社那边同步一下。
t_en: Looks like I'm not needed anymore, right? I'll head off, then. Remember to send us the revisions once you're done so that we can sync up.

t_ch: 哎！一定，一定。至于{NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#小姐}…您应该还是准备亲眼看着在下把结局写完吧。那在下立刻动笔，绝不耽误您的时间。
t_en: Ah, yes, definitely! As for {M#Mister}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}... You're still waiting to see me wrap up the story, right? I'll get writing immediately. I would never dream of wasting your time.

t_ch: 西衍先生开始奋笔疾书，这次他写得眉飞色舞…大概是因为被相中了版权，心情特别好吧。
t_en: Mr. Xiyan begins scribbling furiously, and this time he radiates with delight... He's probably in such good spirits because he's managed to get some cash from this IP.

t_ch: 不一会儿，他昂起自己骄傲的头颅，将稿子给了你。
t_en: Shortly after, he raises his proud head and hands over the manuscript to you.

t_ch: 现在最后一折戏，也就是咱们这个故事的大结局，「仙舟民同悲万古尘，开拓者星落长乐天」也彻底写完了。期待您通读全本之后，能给在下提一些重要的意见。
t_en: Now the final scene, the epic finale of our story, has finally been completed: Xianzhou Laments Woes of Age, Trailblazer Fails Sanctum of Exaltation! I hope you can give me some feedback once you read through it.

t_ch: 嗐！在下差点忘了，今天晚上在下还要和幻戏社的编剧们一起吃饭呢，哈哈，可能这下是真的要发达咯！
t_en: Hey, I almost forgot! I'm having dinner tonight with some of the playwrights from the Mellow-Golden-Mayor Immersia Club. Haha, maybe I really am going to make it on the big stage!

t_ch: 在下要赶紧回趟家，洗洗脸、刮刮胡子、换身衣裳…咱们下次再见吧！
t_en: I have to get home, wash up, shave, change my clothes... So much to do. See you next time!

t_ch: 除此之外，我还随手写了一些您在仙舟各处冒险挑战的外传小故事，您得空可以来我这儿读一读，评说一番，也好让我能把它编篡进咱们这一整出《开拓豪侠传》的话本里。
t_en: Aside from that, I have also written down some side stories of your adventures on the Xianzhou. Feel free to come have a read when you have the time, give some comments, and help me include them into the Legend of the Trailblazer.

t_ch: 有什么新故事吗？
t_en: Any new stories?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#小姐}，《开拓豪侠传》这一整个话本我还在编撰润色呢。
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, I'm still fleshing out the stories in the Legend of the Trailblazer.

t_ch: 顺带我又编了些外传故事，您有没有兴趣试听？
t_en: By the way, I made up a few side stories. Would you like to hear them?

t_ch: 恶党，纳命来吧！
t_en: Evildoers, meet thy maker!

t_ch: 药材在炉鼎中翻滚沸腾着，引得洞天穹顶上阵阵雷霆大作…半晌，「九转升天丹」出炉了。
t_en: The medicinal herbs boiled around in the elixir cauldron, causing thunderclaps to form on the dome above... Soon, the Elixir of Dragon Oil had been concocted.

t_ch: 您快开始为新任龙尊接生吧，马上就是这段故事的高潮部分了！
t_en: You are about to begin delivery of the next high elder. The climax of this story is about to start!

t_ch: 幻胧，是时候算总账了！
t_en: Phantylia, it's time to settle the score once and for all!

t_ch: 来吧，宵小之辈！
t_en: Come and get it, puny worms!

t_ch: 我依稀记得…藏宝图应该就在这附近了……
t_en: I vaguely remember... The treasure map ought to be around here somewhere...

t_ch: 真是辛苦您帮我整理思绪，收集灵感，我这就开动。
t_en: Thank you for helping me sort out my thoughts and get some inspiration. I'll start right away.

t_ch: 西衍先生拿起他的稿本，奋笔疾书。
t_en: Mr. Xiyan took out his notebook and began scribbling at a furious pace.

t_ch: 不一会儿，他志得意满地将稿子给了你。
t_en: Shortly after, he handed over the manuscript to you, filled with satisfaction.

t_ch: 在下已经把「无名客血战寿瘟妖」的部分写好了。
t_en: I've already written the part about "Nameless Battles Author of Plagues."

t_ch: 对喽，这一路咱们的主角——也就是您，历经奇遇，服食灵丹，我得在这「主角模板」上再添几笔，免得下回忘了。
t_en: That's right. Our protagonist on this quest — that is, you — has experienced all these adventures and taken this elixir of life. I have to add a few strokes to this Protagonist Model so that it's not forgotten next time.

t_ch: 说起来，今天走这一遭，我脑袋里多了不少灵感。
t_en: Speaking of which, I was left with tons of inspiration rattling around in my head after today's trip.

t_ch: 我想以您在仙舟上的冒险为蓝本，写些旁支的冒险传奇。把您的主角模板给好好扩充一下，再写个几场<color=#dbc291ff>您的挑战故事</color>，您一定得赏脸听听。
t_en: I'd like to use some of your side adventures on the Xianzhou as material to write some side stories. It'll help flesh out your Protagonist Model, so I'll write out some of <color=#dbc291ff>Your Challenging Adventures</color>. Why don't you listen and tell me what you think?

t_ch: 听说你有特殊委派交给我…
t_en: I heard that you've got a special assignment for me...

t_ch: 哦？想不到艾丝妲站长推荐的特派员，是你这个年轻人。
t_en: Oh? I didn't think the Lead Researcher's task force would be someone as young as you.

t_ch: 也是好事。像你这种局外人，没办法对我的整个大局造成影响……
t_en: But that's fine. An outsider like you has no way of interfering with my master plan...

t_ch: 你一定要说这么多谜语吗？
t_en: Are you done speaking in riddles?

t_ch: 以你的「理解」层次，或许我的这些前辈经验的确是「谜语」吧。
t_en: Based on your level of "understanding," perhaps my seniority and experience really does seem like a "riddle."

t_ch: 所以到底是什么特殊委派？
t_en: So what's the special assignment?

t_ch: 哎，现在的年轻人，真是静不下心来听前辈分享经验。
t_en: Oh, young people these days. They just can't stay still and listen to a senior share their rich experience.

t_ch: 算了，既然你只关心那些无聊的「特殊委派」，那我就给你解释解释吧。
t_en: Forget it. Since you only care about those boring "assignments," let me explain.

t_ch: 空间站的效率你也知道，过了这么久，灾后复兴工作进度还是很缓慢。而那些负责执行的家伙说，自己是因为权限过低无权处理，才会拖这么久。
t_en: I'm sure even you know about efficiency here on the space station. Post-disaster recovery work is still going very slowly. And the guys in charge said it's because their access level is too low, and they don't have the appropriate authority.

t_ch: 而那些有高级权限的科员呢？要么年纪大了不方便亲自出马，要么任务繁重没心思顾及这些小事。所以没办法提供有效支持。
t_en: And what about those researchers with high-level access? They're either too old to help out in person, or they're working on something too huge to bother stooping down to this low level. So there's no way to provide any effective support.

t_ch: 他们这些借口，你懂就好。结果上来说，就是某个管事人向艾丝妲站长提出申请，想要一名符合权限要求的「特派员」，完成这部分特殊的委托——因此，你来了。
t_en: You know, those are just their excuses. At the end of the day, it's basically a supervisor wanting a "special agent" with the right permits to complete those special assignments, and they put in an application with the Lead Researcher for such a person — and that's why you are here.

t_ch: 而很不幸，我被要求负责协助这名「特派员」完成工作，处理一些文件整理的内容。
t_en: And rather unfortunately, I was asked to support this "Task Force" in getting things done as well as to handle all the paperwork.

t_ch: 根据我的整理结果，有一个好消息和一个坏消息要告诉你。
t_en: And based on this paperwork, I've got good news and bad news to tell ya.

t_ch: 好消息是，这些委托其实都不算困难，顶多只是有些麻烦。
t_en: The good news: This assignment isn't hard at all. It's just a slight hassle at worst.

t_ch: 而坏消息是…我手头上临时又被分配了其他的工作。所以我能提供的协助，到此为止。
t_en: And the bad news is... I've been tasked with some other urgent work. So this is about all the support I can offer you for now.

t_ch: 具体的工作内容我已经发送给你了，之后自己看着办吧。
t_en: I've sent the specific work details to you, so you can figure things out yourself later.

t_ch: 特派委托全都完成了！
t_en: All special assignments complete!

t_ch: 哦，辛苦了。酬劳发放的流程不涉及我这边，如果核对之后有任何问题可以直接找艾丝妲站长处理。
t_en: Oh, great job. Remuneration isn't part of my job scope. Check things over and if you have any questions, go ask Lead Researcher Asta directly.

t_ch: 关于特殊委派…
t_en: About the special assignment...

t_ch: 原来你是来跟我说这件事的啊…那我不太有空，抱歉。
t_en: So, that's what you want to talk with me about... Then I don't have the time. Sorry.

t_ch: 哎哟，这位客官，总算把你盼来了。
t_en: Oh, you're finally here, customer!

t_ch: 之前我碰见个热心的云骑姑娘，是你的朋友对吧？
t_en: I met a warmhearted Cloud Knight lady not long ago. She's your friend, right?

t_ch: 是的，她人可好了。
t_en: Yeah, and she's super nice.

t_ch: 哦，你说的是素裳。
t_en: Oh, you must mean Sushang.

t_ch: 嗯，前不久她和商会的小秘书来找过我。
t_en: Yeah, she and Miss Secretary of the merchant guild came by a while ago.

t_ch: 有件事想麻烦你，我想录几段评书放在店里，可是我与那几位先生都不熟，不知能否拜托你替我到现场录上几段？
t_en: Hmm... Can I ask you for a favor? I'm thinking of playing some storytelling shows in my store, but I don't know any of the performers. Would you please go to their live show and record a few pieces for me?

t_ch: 你这是要改行了？
t_en: Do you want to become a storyteller yourself?

t_ch: 不是，我只是想用评书录音来吸引游客。
t_en: No, not at all. I just want to attract more tourists with those recordings.

t_ch: 我跟他们也不算熟。
t_en: I don't know them well, either.

t_ch: 啊？那位云骑军的小姑娘可是向我推荐你来着……
t_en: Really? But that Cloud Knight lady recommended you to me...

t_ch: 哎哟，我只是有些担心，我要是加入商会的物流系统，开始做外卖生意了，那我这儿的老主顾怎么办……
t_en: You know, I'm just worried about my regulars. What if I join the guild's logistics network and start providing takeout food? What will happen to them...?

t_ch: 长此以往，谁还会光顾我这摊子呢？
t_en: Will anyone come to my stall in the long run?

t_ch: 那就不出摊，省力又省心。
t_en: You could shut down the dine-in stall and save the trouble.

t_ch: 那怎么行，这个摊子我支起来多少年了，主打的就是新鲜出炉这一点。要是关掉摊子专职做外卖…这么多年的心血岂不是白费了？
t_en: But this stall has been my bread and butter for years. Freshly baked snacks are my specialty, and if I shut down my stall and do takeout, all those years of hard work will go down the drain, right?

t_ch: 不会的，仙舟上念旧的人多。
t_en: No worries. I'm sure your regulars will still come to your stall.

t_ch: 机巧鸟配送那么方便，仙舟的年轻人都喜欢在家点外卖，以后的事，谁知道呢。
t_en: But cycrane delivery is just so convenient these days. With all those young folks ordering takeout at home, who knows what will happen in the future?

t_ch: 横竖都是赚钱，别在意细节！
t_en: Never mind the details! At the end of the day, it's all about making money, right?

t_ch: 话可不能这么说，钱固然重要，但还不足以与我和老主顾之间的友谊相比。而且，我开摊是想不断精进我的手艺。我要想挣钱，早就找个店当点心师傅去了。
t_en: That's not the case. To me, my relationship with my regulars is way more important than just making money. Plus, I started this stall to hone my skills, not just to earn a buck. If I really wanted to make a kill, I could have worked as a snack master at a fancy restaurant.

t_ch: 我听说素裳姑娘说，阿来和阿往是曲艺界的新星，放我家这摊子上说上几段也不算失礼。
t_en: Sushang mentioned Back'n & Forth to me. They're up-and-comers in the storytelling industry, and I think they'd be a great fit for my stall.

t_ch: 倒是那西衍先生，他可是说书的名家大拿，要是能拿到他的录音，就算是老主顾也没有什么可抱怨的！麻烦你帮我去录一下试试吧！
t_en: And let's not forget Mr. Xiyan, a well-known storyteller. My regulars would love his recordings! Please, I really could use your help!

t_ch: 我听说那西衍先生，还有阿来和阿往两位先生，都是罗浮上擅说书的好手，要是能录上他们的评书，我这客流量肯定不愁！麻烦你帮我去录一下吧！
t_en: I heard that Mr. Xiyan and Back'n & Forth are famous storytellers on this ship. Their recordings will definitely attract new customers to my stall! Please, help me get their recordings!

t_ch: （征得他们的录音授权）
t_en: (Ask their permission to make recordings.)

t_ch: 唷，合着观众老爷今天不想当观众了。
t_en: Alas, looks like our audience has some other ideas today.

t_ch: 嗐，损别人可以，损观众老爷可不行。传出去定让人笑话咱俩小年轻的不会办事。
t_en: Come on, it's one thing to joke about others and a whole different thing to joke about our audience. If we do that, we'll end up becoming the joke in this business.

t_ch: 没错，耳朵不尖——
t_en: Yeah, you laugh at your customer...

t_ch: 眼高于天！
t_en: And you end up a bummer!

t_ch: 尾巴无毛——
t_en: You make an ill-timed joke...

t_ch: 办事不牢！等等，打住打住，咱俩可都没尾巴，合着这属于是见人先来个大马趴，当给观众老爷的见面礼了！
t_en: And you'll be broke! Wait, wait, we're not making terrible jokes. We're straying from the point, so let's get back to the folks!

t_ch: 要不就录刚才这段吧！
t_en: How about I record the crosstalk improv you just had?

t_ch: 还是算了吧，方才那段内容，和咱俩自身不贴合，而且没什么意思。
t_en: Nah, forget it. It's terrible, not catchy at all.

t_ch: 你俩能给个准信吗？
t_en: Can you guys give me a definite answer?

t_ch: 您别急，我俩得商量一下才行。
t_en: Not so fast. We'll have to talk about it.

t_ch: 现在观众老爷想要个授权…
t_en: Now your audience wants a "yes" answer...

t_ch: 好说好说，方才是即兴演出，我们定会给您一个答复。
t_en: Don't worry. We were just improvising, and you'll get an answer from us soon.

t_ch: 好了，这即兴段子还得改，我们先不谈这个，谈谈正事。我觉得录音的事听上去没啥问题，不过我一个人说了可不算，还得问问我搭档的意见。
t_en: There's still much room for our improvisation, but let's put it on hold for now. The recording thing sounds good to me, but I need my partner's opinion before making a final decision.

t_ch: 我觉着…这事恐怕不得行咯。
t_en: Well... I'm not sure about that...

t_ch: 为啥啊，这位可是咱们的老观众了，你就算不同意，高低得给个正当理由不是，光驳人家面子，倒显得咱俩无情无义了。
t_en: But why not? It's just doing our regular customer a favor, right? A flat-out rejection will make us seem heartless.

t_ch: 嗐，咱也不是那薄情寡义之人，咱们也算是熟人，我就直说了吧——我和阿来呢，是相声爱好者，资历尚浅，也没什么拿得出手的作品。
t_en: It's not like I want to reject your request outright, but Back'n and I are just crosstalk lovers. We don't have enough experience to put on a great show yet.

t_ch: ——有是有，也就那么几段编排好的，比我兜里的钱还干净。
t_en: We've put on a few decent shows, but that's all about it. To be frank, we've had fewer successful performances than the coins in my pocket.

t_ch: 给这金人巷高阿姨的摊子录音，目的是增加客流量，但我可不敢打包票，咱俩的相声能吸引到多少人流。
t_en: I just can't guarantee that our crosstalk comedy will really attract any customers for Tall Auntie's stall in Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 就是，别瞧着咱们哥俩年纪比你大，但讲相声这事也不算经验丰富，要是先在潜在观众那儿留下什么负面印象，那多不好。
t_en: Yeah, we might be older than you, but we don't have much more experience in crosstalk performance. We just don't want to leave any negative impressions on our potential audience.

t_ch: 要不你俩去现场表演表演？
t_en: Why don't you guys do live performances instead?

t_ch: 哪有那个时间呐，我俩只是相声爱好者，工作日咱俩还得在码头干活呢。
t_en: Because we simply don't have the time. We're just crosstalk lovers, and we've got work to do at the dock on work days.

t_ch: 放心，报酬少不了你们的。
t_en: Don't worry. You will be generously paid.

t_ch: 瞧您说的，我像是稀罕报酬的人吗？咱们可都是有正经工作的。
t_en: Hah, do we look like we are solely motivated by money? We've got our own jobs to do.

t_ch: 我是他发小兼同事，他这人我最了解了，相声本就图一乐，他自个儿打心底可不觉得能靠这档子事挣钱发财。
t_en: I've known him since I was a kid, and I know him well. He gets into the crosstalk industry for fun, and he doesn't really expect us to hit it big with our performances.

t_ch: 我觉得是你们想太多了…
t_en: I think you guys are overthinking things...

t_ch: 咱们这还在上升期，凡事自然得谨慎才好。
t_en: As newcomers in this industry, we can't be too careful with our new career.

t_ch: 您呐，也高低体谅一下我俩的难处，就甭为难咱俩了。
t_en: Yeah, please understand our concerns and don't make things too difficult for us.

t_ch: 还是请回吧，要是想听段子，我们倒是无任欢迎。
t_en: Please don't bring up the recording thing again, but if you ever want to listen to our crosstalk, you're always welcome here.

t_ch: （他俩态度这么坚决…哎，直接去西衍先生那儿碰碰运气吧。）
t_en: (There is no room for negotiation in this matter, it seems. Maybe I should just try my luck with Mr. Xiyan.)

t_ch: （征得他的录音授权）
t_en: (Ask his permission to make recordings.)

t_ch: 原来如此，好说好说。我原本就给「不夜侯」准备了些话本，你们那边想用尽管录就是了。
t_en: I see your point. Actually I've prepared some shows for the Sleepless Earl. Feel free to record them if you want.

t_ch: （录制西衍先生的评书）
t_en: (Record Mr. Xiyan's storytelling shows.)

t_ch: 西衍先生声情并茂地录了几段评书……
t_en: Mr. Xiyan puts a lot of emotion into his performance...

t_ch: 能多些听众总是好的，没落的艺术总得有条活路。
t_en: It's always great to have more listeners. An art form that's in decline deserves a chance to survive.

t_ch: 嗯？你怎么在叹气，莫不是在下方才的评书出了什么问题？还是说你有什么别的问题？
t_en: Hmm? Why are you sighing? Is there anything wrong with my performance? Or are you worried about something else?

t_ch: （向他讲述被阿来阿往拒绝一事）
t_en: (Tell him that you were rejected by Back'n & Forth.)

t_ch: 相声啊，和说书一样渐渐没落了，他们这么谨慎也情有可原，毕竟这年头光靠说相声可养不活自己。
t_en: Well, crosstalk, just like storytelling, seems to be on the decline these days. I totally understand why they are so cautious. After all, crosstalk shows aren't exactly a reliable source of income anymore.

t_ch: 不过他们到底是新人，经验不足，思维陷入误区倒也正常。你要是录制他们的相声，在下倒是有个法子能将他俩劝回来。
t_en: But they're new and inexperienced, so it's natural for them to have misunderstandings about the industry. If you want to record their crosstalk shows, I have a way to persuade them.

t_ch: 愿闻其详。
t_en: Please elaborate.

t_ch: 此事说来简单，你只是被他们拉入他们的惯性思维里去了。
t_en: It's simple, actually. You were just pulled into their way of thinking.

t_ch: 请指导我！
t_en: Please enlighten me!

t_ch: 不敢当不敢当，在下也只是有些经验之谈。
t_en: I wouldn't go so far as to say I'm going to "enlighten" you, but I do have a thing or two to share with you.

t_ch: …要不我俩录一段相声吧？
t_en: ...How about us two record a crosstalk show together?

t_ch: 我见这次初试啼声也是个机会，咱们还是留给那两位相声新人吧。
t_en: Hmm, perhaps not. This would be a great opportunity for the two up-and-coming crosstalk comedians.

t_ch: 无论是相声还是说书，都是得面向观众不断训练。这就跟云骑军的日常训练一样，得多跑多练。
t_en: Whether it's crosstalk or storytelling, it's all about constant practice in front of the audience. It's just like the Cloud Knights' daily training.

t_ch: 他俩局限在一个地方表演，反而不是好事——不过他们还有工作嘛，情有可原。其实说白了，相声就得不断接受观众的反馈，不断改进。
t_en: Always performing in the same place can actually be detrimental to their growth, although I understand that they still have jobs to do. Honing crosstalk skills is all about accepting feedback from the audience and making constant improvements.

t_ch: 有了这录音表演，反倒是好事，这意味着会有更多的「听众」。他们大可以反过来利用听众来试验自己的新段子，定期回访听听大家的意见，总比闭门造车好。
t_en: Recording their performance could be beneficial for them, as it means they'll have a bigger audience to test out their new materials and get more feedback. This is way better than talking to themselves!

t_ch: 确实！
t_en: That's true, indeed!

t_ch: 确实…哎，不对，我确实个什么劲儿。抱歉，在下方才嘴瓢了一下，请见谅。
t_en: Indeed... Ugh, my bad, I didn't mean to come off as arrogant. Sorry about that.

t_ch: 有道理，我这就去找他们。
t_en: That makes sense. I'll talk with them again.

t_ch: 嗯，不过你先别急，我还有话没说完。
t_en: Hang on. There's one more thing I'd like to say.

t_ch: 方才说的你记下了吧？你直接跟他们解释这个原理，他们自然会懂的。
t_en: You remember what I said, right? Just tell my words to them and they'll understand.

t_ch: 若是还不松口，那你就报上我的名头，说金人巷那边要是想开我的话本专场，想借他们的相声录音来热热场子。
t_en: And if they're still hesitant, just tell them that I'm planning to start a storytelling show in Aurum Alley, and that I'd like to use their crosstalk as a warm-up act.

t_ch: （转述西衍先生的说辞）
t_en: (Tell them Mr. Xiyan's words.)

t_ch: 有道理，他说得对，也怪我，我怎么就没想到呢。
t_en: Hmm, yeah, he's got a point. I should've realized that sooner.

t_ch: 亡羊补牢也不算晚，况且，能给西衍先生热场子，这可是想都不敢想的机会啊。
t_en: It's never too late to make things right. Plus, I'd do anything for a chance to give a warm-up performance for Mr. Xiyan.

t_ch: 要是我夜里梦见的，第二天醒来保管哈喇子淌满一枕头。
t_en: If I ever got a chance to do this in my dreams, I'd wake myself up from laughing so hard.

t_ch: 那你还不赶紧答应。
t_en: Then what are we waiting for?

t_ch: 行！咱俩这就来秀几段绝活——
t_en: Sure thing! Let's do this!

t_ch: （录制阿来阿往的相声）
t_en: (Record Back'n & Forth's performance.)

t_ch: 等赶明儿有空了，我俩一定会登门拜访一下，顺带么，也收集一波听众的反馈。
t_en: We'll swing by Tall Auntie's stall when we're free and get some feedback from our audience.

t_ch: 好勒，您慢点走，回去替我们给店主阿姨说声谢谢嗷。
t_en: Yeah, take your time, and please convey our thanks to her.

t_ch: 高阿姨把我叫了过来，看来高阿姨与码头建立合作一事，进展顺利？
t_en: Since you called me over, Tall Auntie, looks like you've agreed to cooperate with the dock, I assume?

t_ch: 顺利顺利，我还麻烦了{NICKNAME}去问录音的事，不知道进展如何了？
t_en: Yeah, I have. I wonder if {NICKNAME} has brought me the recordings I needed?

t_ch: 我拿到你要的录音了。
t_en: I've got the recordings you asked for.

t_ch: 哎哟，太感谢了！稍等我先试试……
t_en: Thank you so much! Let me try it out...

t_ch: 高阿姨将录音放入播放设备中。
t_en: Tall Auntie puts the recording into her playback device.

t_ch: 辛苦你了，有了这玩意的帮忙，客流量一定少不了。
t_en: Thank you for your help! With these recordings, we'll definitely get more customers.

t_ch: 我所担忧的事已经被解决，那我自然得说到做到。麻烦小秘书那个…物流规划，是这个说法没错吧？高阿姨记性不好。
t_en: Now that my worries have been resolved, I've got to keep my promise. It's about... oh, what was it again... logistics planning, right? Apologies, I have a terrible memory.

t_ch: 没错，就是这个，既然高阿姨都这么说了，那就麻烦{NICKNAME}将「小吃铺」纳入到物流规划之中吧，这样码头就会有新的运单了。
t_en: Yeah, that's the thing. Since Tall Auntie already mentioned it, then {NICKNAME}, please add the Food Stall into the logistics plan. This way, the docks can get some new orders soon.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。对了，我听素裳姑娘说，他们几位还有不少作品，你要是感兴趣啊，可以到他们那儿再瞧瞧。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. By the way, Sushang told me that those performers have prepared more shows. Feel free to drop by and check them out if you're interested.

t_ch: 在你的帮助下，光顾小吃摊的人越来越多。
t_en: Thanks to your aid, more and more people are visiting the Food Stall.

t_ch: 老板为何面露难色？
t_en: You look upset. What happened?

t_ch: 这顾客的心意真是难懂——呃，我不是说你，抱歉，只是有感而发。
t_en: Customers are truly a beast to try and understand — oh, I'm not referring to you, sorry. I just had to vent.

t_ch: 明曦姑娘来找过我说过商会的事，我大体上是支持的，能把生意做大是好事，但也得谨慎行事。所以我就去做了个市场调研，收集潜在顾客的口味喜好。
t_en: Mingxi shared her thoughts on the merchant guild with me, and I am in agreement with most of her ideas. It's always a good thing to make the pie bigger, right? So I decided to do some market research to get a better sense of what my potential customers are into.

t_ch: 可这结果一收集出来，我发现大家的口味真是古怪，两极分化极为严重，要么就嫌「烈焰浓茶」茶味淡，要么就是说「烈焰浓茶」不够「烈」。
t_en: But after collecting the results, I realized that their tastes are really polarized. Either they think my Flaming Potent Tea tastes too bland or too strong.

t_ch: 你可以研发几种不同的。
t_en: Why don't you develop several flavors?

t_ch: 哈，这就是典型的外行人思维，我是打算调整配方不假，可这配方比例怎么调？
t_en: Ha! That's a typical layperson's suggestion. I do plan on tweaking my recipe, but I'm not sure how to do it yet.

t_ch: 我这小本买卖，研发成本有限，「几种」真是说得轻松……
t_en: I'm running a small business and my budget is limited. I can't just "develop several flavors"...

t_ch: 那你打算听谁？
t_en: So, which part of the customers are you going to listen to?

t_ch: 哈，这就是我所头疼的地方了——顾客的意见都很重要，但是要听谁的才好呢？
t_en: See, that's what gives me a headache! Customers' opinions are important, but whose opinions should I prioritize?

t_ch: 不烈？往里怼酒精啊！
t_en: Too bland? Just add some alcohol!

t_ch: 哎！哎哎哎，您怎么把我心里话说出来了！
t_en: Hey! Hey, hey! That's exactly what I would be doing if I could!

t_ch: 但是对不住，地衡司颁发的经营许可还有商品税率注定了咱这儿卖的只能是茶，您明白的对吧？
t_en: But unfortunately, I can't. According to the regulations issued by the Realm-Keeping Commission, we can only sell tea to customers. You surely know that, right?

t_ch: 相逢即是缘，干脆就找阁下做次口味测试吧，也算是名正言顺地请你喝两杯。
t_en: But now that you're here, why not try my new recipes? Feel free to enjoy them. It's on the house.

t_ch: 我正巧按不同的配比，研制了三种全新的「烈焰浓茶」，还请你来试试，然后告诉我你的感受。
t_en: I happen to have developed three new recipes for Flaming Potent Tea in different ratios. Please try them and tell me how you feel.

t_ch: 杜老板端来了一杯「烈焰浓茶」。
t_en: Boss Du brings you a mug of Flaming Potent Tea.

t_ch: 这杯的比例是九比一，为那些嫌「烈焰浓茶」不够「烈」的客户调制而成。
t_en: This one, with a ratio of nine to one, is prepared for those who think my tea is too bland.

t_ch: 你且喝下去试试——
t_en: Give it a sip...

t_ch: 你将茶一饮而尽，辛辣的口感令你怀疑你喉咙是否在燃烧。
t_en: As you chug down the tea, its spicy taste sets your throat ablaze.

t_ch: 你闭上眼使劲忍耐，那股火热的焦灼感滑落至胃部，过了许久，你才终于适应了这种强烈的肠胃道刺激感。
t_en: You close your eyes and try to hold back the burning sensation that slides down to your stomach. It takes a long time before you finally get used to the irritation in your stomach.

t_ch: 你睁开眼睛，强烈刺激带来的生理泪水令你眼眶通红，你忽然发现眼前的一切失去了原有的光鲜。巨鲸在你脑海中歌唱…下浮…跳跃…旋转，直至眼前突然漆黑一片。
t_en: You open your eyes, red from the tears caused by the intense sensations. All the colors in the world fade before your eyes. You hear the song of a giant whale in your head. You feel yourself floating... jumping... and spinning... until everything suddenly goes black in your vision.

t_ch: 好了，世界总算清静下来了，这是你倒下前脑海中闪过的最后一丝念头——
t_en: "Well, at least the world has become quiet again..." That is the last thought that flashes through your mind before you collapse.

t_ch: 吓死我了，我以为你出什么事了，刚才你忽然喝着喝着就倒了。
t_en: Oh, you're awake! Thank goodness! You just passed out while drinking the tea! I thought you kicked the bucket or something!

t_ch: 正打算把你往店门口外搬呢…还好你醒了，看来没什么大碍了。
t_en: I was trying to move you out of my tea house... but now that you've woken up, I guess everything is okay.

t_ch: 这么说来，方才那方子是要不得了。来，再尝尝这个，我换了道七比三的方子，保管不会出事！
t_en: Looks like that recipe is not going to work. Well, I've got another one for you to try! This one has a ratio of seven to three, so it's totally safe!

t_ch: 吸取了前一杯的教训，你浅尝了一口，辛辣的口感依旧存在，但随即又被淡淡的茶叶清香味遮掩。
t_en: Having learned your lesson, you take a sip of the new tea. While it's still a bit intense, the taste is quickly overtaken by its aromatic tea flavor.

t_ch: 你观察了数息，发现喝起来口感顺滑，并无不适，你忍不住一口一口地喝下去。
t_en: You wait for a while and don't feel any discomfort, so you start chugging it down.

t_ch: 你觉得自己此刻舒爽极了，像在柔软的云朵间漂浮，仰卧，来回起坐。你捧起一朵云砸向你的同伴三月七……
t_en: You feel incredibly comfortable almost as if you were floating among soft clouds. You grab a cluster of clouds and playfully toss it at your companion March 7th...

t_ch: 「等等，三月七？」你意识到不对，猛地抬头望向眼前人，却意外发现茶庄的老板竟有好几位之多，他们长得一模一样，就连说话声也完全一致。
t_en: "Wait, March 7th?" At that moment, you realize that something is wrong, shooting a questioning glance at the tea house owner standing in front of you. To your astonishment, you see several identical-looking owners, all speaking to you in the exact same tone.

t_ch: 「可是为何我先前没发现…他竟有这么多孪生兄弟？」你为自己的观察力之低下而恼怒，你不明白为什么这么明显的事你此前竟从未留意过。
t_en: "But why didn't I notice earlier that he had so many siblings?" Frustrated by your lack of observation, you don't understand why you've never noticed something so obvious before.

t_ch: 你开始迷茫，继而是某种不可名状的后怕与懊恼，你不明白除此以外，仙舟上还有多少秘密摆在你眼前却又无法察觉，这究竟是你的失误，亦或是旁人的精心安排……
t_en: Your mind is filled with bewilderment, followed by an unexplainable sense of fear and regret. You can't help but contemplate how many secrets might be right in front of your eyes yet remain unnoticed by you. Is this truly because of your lack of observation? Or could it be that someone else is pulling the strings...?

t_ch: 醒一醒，你还听得到吗？{NICKNAME}？这是几，你看得到吗？
t_en: Wake up, {NICKNAME}! Are you still with me? How many fingers do you see?

t_ch: 醒了就好醒了就好，没想到你这么……
t_en: Ah, finally, you've woken up. I didn't expect that you would just...

t_ch: 哎算了，不提这个了，这七比三的方子又失败咯。我给你换了个最低配比五五开的新品，你来尝尝？最后一杯了，要是还不行我就放弃研发新品了。
t_en: Ah, forget it. So, this recipe is another failure, it seems. Well... how about the last one, a drink with only a one to one ratio? If this fails, too, I might as well give up developing new products.

t_ch: 你慢慢地将这杯「烈焰浓茶」喝下肚，先前的两次新品已经令你吸取了足够多的教训。
t_en: Having learned so much from the previous two mugs, you take your time and slowly drink this last mug of Flaming Potent Tea.

t_ch: 你清晰地感受着它的口感，一半像泉水，甘甜可口，一半像是火焰，灼热滚烫。两者合二为一，顺着食道渐渐下落，温润而平静。
t_en: It tastes like a miraculous mix of sweet spring water and searing flame. The calm yet warm liquid slowly descends your esophagus, providing a comforting sensation.

t_ch: 你抬头看了看杜老板，不确定，又瞥了瞥。这才敢确定你没有出现任何幻觉，现在你终于可以长舒一口气——你确信这就是最适合你的「烈焰浓茶」配比。
t_en: Uncertain, you throw a few more glances at Boss Du, making sure that you aren't hallucinating. Finally, you let out a sigh of relief — you are pretty sure that this is the best Flaming Potent Tea recipe for you.

t_ch: 怎么样，你感觉如何？要是有哪里不舒服记得要告诉我，实在不行我去把丹鼎司的医士喊过来。
t_en: How are you feeling? Let me know if you feel bad in any way, and I'll call the healers from the Alchemy Commission if I have to.

t_ch: 不用了，我感觉挺好的。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm feeling fine.

t_ch: 真的吗，那就好。
t_en: Really? Good to know.

t_ch: 我从未如此平静过。
t_en: I've never been so calm.

t_ch: …我倒不知道我们家「烈焰浓茶」还有这种效果，发出这种评价的…你是头一个。
t_en: ...Well, I didn't know my Flaming Potent Tea could have such an effect. You're the first to make... such a remark.

t_ch: 咦，这么看来这个比例是没问题的。我明白了，之后的新品我就在这个比例上慢慢调整口味和配方吧。
t_en: Looks like this recipe is just right. I see. I'll make further adjustments based on this one, then.

t_ch: 谢谢你啊，{NICKNAME}。有你在我这儿亲身试茶，我这才有底气去研发新品。我看小秘书已经过来了，你们那个物流规划，要怎么做来着？
t_en: Thank you for trying out my new recipes, {NICKNAME}. Thanks to you, now I've plucked my courage to develop new products. Oh, looks like Miss Secretary has arrived! So, what are your plans about logistics planning?

t_ch: 我看你们喝得开心，根本就没注意到我…哎，那就麻烦{NICKNAME}将「杜氏茶庄」纳入到物流规划之中吧，这样码头就会有新的运单了。
t_en: You were too focused on enjoying tea to notice me... {NICKNAME}, please add Du's Teahouse into the logistics plan so the docks can get some new orders.

t_ch: 哈哈，我这茶庄别没有，就是茶水多。很高兴与商会建立正式的合作。
t_en: Haha, can't argue with that. Everyone loves my tea after all. Anyway, I'm glad to formally establish a cooperation with the merchant guild.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，希望我们接下来也能合作愉快，让更多的人尝到茶庄的烈焰浓茶。
t_en: Absolutely! I hope that our cooperation will be fruitful and bring your tasty Flaming Potent Tea to an even larger customer base.

t_ch: 我听商会的明曦姑娘提起过你，你们是打算用码头把各家店的货发到其他地方去。
t_en: I heard about you from Mingxi of the Merchant Guild. You're planning to use the docks to ship the stores' goods to other delves.

t_ch: 像我们茶庄，做外卖生意是最合适不过的，不过……
t_en: For stores like my teahouse, doing business by way of delivery is pretty appropriate. However...

t_ch: 在你的帮助下，光顾杜氏茶庄的人越来越多。
t_en: Thanks to your aid, more and more people are visiting Du's Teahouse.

t_ch: 这家口碑一般……
t_en: This store doesn't have fantastic ratings...

t_ch: 你是{NICKNAME}对吧，正巧我有件事……
t_en: You are {NICKNAME}, right? It just so happens that I have something...

t_ch: 我们好像也不是很熟吧。
t_en: Wait, have we ever met before?

t_ch: 哎，贵人多忘事，我先前在小秘书那儿见过你几面，不过你可能没什么印象了……
t_en: Yeah, we've met a few times at Miss Secretary's office, but perhaps you don't remember me anymore...

t_ch: 是我，你有什么事吗？
t_en: Oh, it's you. How can I help?

t_ch: 太好了，我有件小事想麻烦你，不过你放心，托人办事总得有点表示，这点人情世故我还是懂的。
t_en: To be honest, I was hoping you could do me a small favor. Don't worry. I'll make it worth your while.

t_ch: 打住，我很忙。
t_en: Hold it. I'm busy.

t_ch: 呃，不会占用你很多时间的，你要不再考虑一下？
t_en: Well, I won't take up much of your time. Maybe you'd like to reconsider?

t_ch: 是这样的，我家这店原是卖文玩的，但这些年来总有化外民来我店里「淘宝」，说什么在这里低价淘到了古董。
t_en: Here's the thing: My store sells fandangles, but lately, more and more Outworlders have been flooding in, claiming they've bought precious antiques at low prices.

t_ch: 久而久之，大家就把我这店当成是古董店了。商会的人也劝我尽早转型，我寻思这里头的利润确实大，做成一单就能歇息好久，可比卖文玩清闲。
t_en: Over time, people have started to see my store as an antique shop, and even the merchant guild has been recommending that I officially make the switch. To be honest, selling antiques is more lucrative than selling fandangles, and it would give me more time to relax.

t_ch: 但是要转型，就等于重新开业，想把店铺的名头打响可不容易，我想了半天也没想出个所以然来。
t_en: The thing is, if I were to make the switch, it would be like opening a whole new shop, and I'd have to start from scratch and build up a new customer base. I've been thinking about it, but I haven't quite figured out how to do it yet.

t_ch: 最后还是小秘书给我出了个主意，她这计划挺好，就是需要个人来搭把手。
t_en: In the end, Miss Secretary came up with a great idea, and now I need someone to help me execute it.

t_ch: 所以你就找到了我？
t_en: That's why you've come to me?

t_ch: 嗐，我本来是想找素裳姑娘帮忙的，但我看她也挺忙的，只好来麻烦你了。
t_en: Well, I was planning to ask Sushang for help, but she was too busy. So I was wondering if I could perhaps trouble you instead?

t_ch: 我哪里懂这些…
t_en: But I know nothing about antiques...

t_ch: 这个很简单的，若不是小机巧鸟做不来，我也不至于麻烦你了。
t_en: It's simple. I wouldn't have asked you if my little cycrane could do it.

t_ch: 转型有风险，商业需谨慎。
t_en: This would be a risky leap. Think twice before you act.

t_ch: 放心好了，试错期又没多长，横竖不过十几年，一眨眼的功夫罢了。要真失败了，那就继续卖文玩呗。
t_en: No worries. It'll only take me a dozen years to try it out and see if this plan works. And if it doesn't, I can always go back to selling fandangles.

t_ch: 其实我先前已经试营业过一段时间，起初客流量还可以，可没过多久，就无人问津了。
t_en: Honestly, I've tried it before, but my customer base just slowly disappeared over time.

t_ch: 我原来没当回事，可渐渐的在我们文玩圈子里也听到了些风声，我出去一打听，嗐——
t_en: At first, I thought it was just bad luck, but then the same thing happened in my fandangles circle as well. So I started asking around, and I found out... tsk...

t_ch: 这帮子化外民一会儿说我卖假货，一会儿说我不懂行，一传十十传百的，我这店名声就臭了。
t_en: It turns out that some ignorant Outworlders have been spreading rumors that I don't know anything about antiques and that I've been selling fake antiques. That's how my reputation got tarnished.

t_ch: 你该不会真的卖假货吧？
t_en: You're not selling fake antiques, are you?

t_ch: 我怎么会干这种事，就算我敢这么做，地衡司也不会放过我的，在咱们仙舟上开店可得遵守规章制度。
t_en: Come on! How could I even do that? Even if I wanted to, the Realm-Keeping Commission wouldn't let me get away with it. All the stores on the Xianzhou ship have to follow the rules and regulations.

t_ch: 你看着的确不像懂行的。
t_en: Well, you do give off the impression that you know nothing about antiques.

t_ch: 你这人怎么说话的，面相又不能反应一个人的学问。
t_en: Hey! It's unfair to judge someone solely based on their appearance.

t_ch: 干脆结业，回家继承家产去。
t_en: Why don't you just close your shop and inherit your family fortune?

t_ch: 这怎么行呢，我不开店我就连接待同好的地儿都没有了。
t_en: If I were to do that, I wouldn't even have a place for my fellow curio enthusiasts.

t_ch: 哎，你是有所不知了，我一个土生土长的仙舟人，一开始就没搞懂化外民口中的「古董」到底是个什么定义。
t_en: You just don't get it. I mean, I was born and raised on this Xianzhou ship, and I just can't wrap my head around how those Outworlders define "antiques."

t_ch: 几十年的文玩，工艺又新又好，他们认为这不是古董。可那几百年的文玩，我看着不是掉漆就是缺胳膊少腿的，我卖之前还得重新喷漆上色，他们倒觉得我在糟蹋古董。
t_en: Some fandangles made a few decades ago can have amazing craftsmanship, but those outsiders don't even consider them to be true antiques. Meanwhile, some fandangles made centuries ago are so dilapidated that I have to repaint them to make them presentable, but then those same outsiders accuse me of destroying antiques.

t_ch: 我就纳闷了，几十年和一两百年有什么区别，不都是弹指一挥间嘛。
t_en: I mean, what's the big deal between antiques from decades ago and centuries ago? It's all just a fleeting amount of time, isn't it?

t_ch: 还好，明曦姑娘听闻我这差点砸了招牌，过来帮了我个大忙——她带我去借来了谛听，靠这玩意侦测文玩的年限，我正好也练练眼力。
t_en: Thankfully, Mingxi heard about my predicament and helped me. She lent me a Diting that allows me to figure out the origin year of my fandangles.

t_ch: 现在眼力劲儿我是有了，就是还缺点人气——所以，我打算在店门口这儿搞一个「真假鉴宝」的活动。
t_en: Now all I need is a little boost in popularity, and that's why I'm planning to host a treasure appraisal event right in front of my store.

t_ch: 简单来说就是，把待鉴定的古董摆在门口，用谛听来识别具体的年限。年限短的属于假古董，那就直接砸烂，最后留下真古董。
t_en: Here's the plan: I'll place the antiques to be appraised at the door and use Diting to figure out their origin year. If they were made just a few decades ago, I'll smash them. That way, only antiques made centuries ago would be left in my store.

t_ch: 现在嘛，我想请你帮个忙，来先预排一次节目，好让我心里有个底儿。
t_en: Now I need you to help me with the rehearsal so that I'll know what the real event will be like.

t_ch: 我没有古董，拿什么鉴宝？
t_en: But I don't even have antiques. How am I supposed to appraise them?

t_ch: 这个你不用担心，都包在我身上，我从库房里整理了一堆库存出来，有古董也有普通文玩，我们家多少年的积累了，量大管饱！
t_en: No need to worry about that. I've brought out a whole bunch of antiques and ordinary fandangles from my family's warehouse, so you'll have plenty to smash at the upcoming show!

t_ch: 怎么说，砸就完事了？
t_en: So, all I need to do is to smash them all?

t_ch: 别别别，你别急，虽然是得砸，但是你得用谛听找到真正的古董才行。
t_en: No, no. You'll need to use Diting to find out the true antiques and smash only the fake ones.

t_ch: 等等，我先把「真假鉴宝」节目的宝贝请出来——
t_en: Hold on a second. Let me show you the antiques we'll be using in the event.

t_ch: 说再多都是虚的，你上手实践一下就知道是怎么回事了——
t_en: Try it yourself and you'll see what it's all about.

t_ch: 我找了不少关系，这才借来了谛听。这小家伙能测定物件的年限，用来鉴别古董真假是再合适不过了。
t_en: Here, I got this Diting through some connections. This little guy will help you figure out the origin year of objects, so you'll know which antiques to smash.

t_ch: 有了它，你就能即刻看到结果。说再多都是虚的，你上手实践一下就知道是怎么回事了。
t_en: Follow its guidance and you won't make mistakes. Well, just try it yourself and you'll see what to do.

t_ch: 来，你对着这两个罐子开启谛听的鉴定功能试试——
t_en: Activate your Diting and use its "Appraise" feature on these jars.

t_ch: 鉴定开启后，物件本身起了反应的就是真的，毫无反应的就是假的。
t_en: While "Appraise" is activated, the jar that responds is the genuine one, and the other is the fake one.

t_ch: 好了，现在你该知道哪个是真古董，哪个是假货了吧？
t_en: Well, now you should know which is the genuine antique and which is fake, right?

t_ch: 现在，把那个假的砸掉，真的别碰啊！
t_en: Now, smash the fake one. And don't touch the genuine one!

t_ch: 好了好了，剩下那个真的你别动啊。
t_en: All right, all right. Just leave the genuine one alone.

t_ch: 现在时机正好，我去喊点人来，希望这次「鉴宝」活动能圆满举行吧……
t_en: It's about time to get this show on the road. I'll gather up some folks here. Fingers crossed that this event will be a success...

t_ch: 打着鉴宝节目的噱头，泊阳成功招徕了几位潜在顾客。
t_en: With the gimmick of a treasure appraisal event, Boyang has attracted a few potential customers.

t_ch: 这是什么节目？「公司」的真人秀吗？
t_en: What's with the setup? An IPC reality show?

t_ch: 好像是罗浮本地节目吧，应该和公司的电视台没关系。
t_en: No, I don't think it has anything to do with IPC. A local Luofu event, it seems.

t_ch: 俗话都说，真的假不了，假的真不了。本次「鉴宝」活动由我们「清玩斋」独家举办，各位观众，有请我们的鉴宝人{NICKNAME}——
t_en: As the saying goes, the fake can never be real! This treasure appraisal event is hosted by Elegance Pagoda! Now, here comes our appraiser, {NICKNAME} —

t_ch: （看样子现在该轮到我大显身手了。）
t_en: (Now it's time to show what I'm made of!)

t_ch: 嚯，鉴定为——假！请记住，我们「清玩斋」对假货一直保持零容忍的态度！
t_en: Whew, this one is... fake! Just a friendly reminder that here at Elegance Pagoda, we have a zero-tolerance policy for fakes!

t_ch: 这究竟是怎么看出来的，神奇！
t_en: This is amazing! How did the appraiser spot that?

t_ch: 诶诶诶？快住手快住手！
t_en: Wait... what are you doing? Stop it! STOP!

t_ch: 笑死，节目效果来了。
t_en: Hahaha, now this is starting to get fun!

t_ch: …咳咳，看来我们的「鉴宝人」有些激动了。大家放心，委托人提供的物件如有遭遇类似情况，我们一定会照价补偿…补偿从「鉴宝人」的劳务费里扣！
t_en: ...Ahem, it seems our "appraiser" got a bit too carried away. But rest assured, if we accidentally break a genuine antique, we will definitely compensate for it... and we'll be taking the cost out of our appraiser's paycheck!

t_ch: 挺有意思的，但愿别太贵……
t_en: Sounds interesting. I hope their antiques aren't too expensive...

t_ch: 等会儿去逛逛也好。
t_en: Yeah, we can go check it out later.

t_ch: 太感谢你了，刚才真是多亏有你啊。
t_en: Oh, I just can't thank you enough.

t_ch: 辛苦你了，有了鉴宝活动，我们店的名声总算是有救了，之后的生意想必也会好转起来。
t_en: Thanks to your performance, my reputation has taken a turn for the better. I'm sure the business will improve over time.

t_ch: 我刚才也看得津津有味的，泊阳老板也和我这边谈好了合作事宜，事不宜迟，那就麻烦{NICKNAME}将「清玩斋」纳入到物流规划之中吧，这样码头就会有新的运单了。
t_en: That was fantastic. Boyang and I have worked out all the details of our cooperation. {NICKNAME}, please add Elegance Pagoda into the logistics plan so the docks can get some new orders.

t_ch: 哎哟，我说什么来着，就这跟你说话的功夫，玉兆后台上就一直在弹订单消息了。
t_en: Oh, what a pleasant surprise! I've been receiving orders via my jade abacus just as we're talking!

t_ch: 多亏了你和小秘书的帮忙啊，有码头在，发货也方便些…啊抱歉，刚刚在想生意上的事想得太入神了，客人催得急，我先去忙了，回头再聊。
t_en: I'm so grateful to you and Miss Secretary for all your help. With the dock provided by the guild, shipping will become more convenient... Oops, sorry, I got a bit too caught up in business talk. Anyway, I should probably go attend to my customers now. Thanks again, and I'll see you later!

t_ch: 够了！快住手！
t_en: Enough! Stop!

t_ch: 还好没砸到，这可是真货啊，幸亏我提前贴了标签…记住，砸假的！
t_en: Wow, lucky it didn't get smashed. That's the real deal right there. Thank goodness I labeled it earlier... Remember, smash the fake one!

t_ch: 当你回来的时候，恰好碰见了明曦前来拜访。
t_en: As you're returning, you happen to run into Mingxi coming over for a visit.

t_ch: 在你的帮助下，光顾清玩斋的人越来越多。
t_en: Thanks to your aid, more and more people are visiting Elegance Pagoda.

t_ch: 明曦受到小天的邀请而来，恰好听到了你的「激辩」。
t_en: Mingxi arrives at Little Tian's invitation and was just in time to hear your "heated argument."

t_ch: 当你忙完跑堂，回过神来，明曦已经受邀来到美馔阁了。
t_en: When you finally have some downtime after serving the customers, Mingxi had already arrived at Delicacy Pavilion.

t_ch: 当你还在回味「烈焰浓茶」时，明曦已经受邀来到杜氏茶庄了。
t_en: While you were still savoring the Flaming Potent Tea, Mingxi had already arrived at Du's Teahouse.

t_ch: 围观群众渐渐散去，你注意到明曦已经受邀来到清玩斋了。
t_en: As the onlookers gradually scatter, you notice that Mingxi had already arrived at Elegance Pagoda.

t_ch: 站住，我要用我爸爸送给我的超强以太灵打赢你！
t_en: Stop right there! I will defeat you with the super powerful Aether Spirit my dad gave me!

t_ch: 你说…爸爸是不是不想让我玩以太战线啊？
t_en: Do you think... my dad was trying to discourage me from playing Aetherium Wars?

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫在此，前方禁止通行！
t_en: Silvermane Guards, reporting for duty! No passage up ahead!

t_ch: 对不起，我不是铁卫…在现实中当不成银鬃铁卫，就只能在游戏里过把瘾了。
t_en: Sorry, I'm not actually a Silvermane Guard... I couldn't become one in real life, so I can only fantasize in games.

t_ch: 你也是以太战线的玩家吗？跟我来场以太灵对战吧！
t_en: You play Aetherium Wars too? Let's have an Aether Spirit battle!

t_ch: 不要问为什么，对上了眼神就要一决高下，这是以太战线的规矩！
t_en: Don't ask me why! When our eyes meet, we have to battle, that's the rule of Aetherium Wars!

t_ch: 可恶，居然又输了…看来只跟玩家对战是没法变强的，得去刷点游离数据了。
t_en: Drats, I lost again... I'll never get stronger just battling other people, I need to go farm some roaming data.

t_ch: 也给你个建议，如果不想发生对战，就躲着点其他玩家吧。
t_en: Let me give you a suggestion: If you don't want to battle, you should try and avoid other players.

t_ch: 天天在决胜乐园巡逻，大家都在玩以太战线，看得我心都痒了…趁长官不在，跟我来一把对决吧！
t_en: I spend every day patrolling the Victory Zone while everyone plays Aetherium Wars, I can't bear it anymore... The Captain's not here at the moment, you'll battle me, won't you?

t_ch: 我、我这是故意让你赢了，你可要知恩图报，别跟长官告发我啊！
t_en: I—I let you win on purpose! You can repay me by not telling the Captain about this!

t_ch: 等等，让我研究一下你的以太灵吧！
t_en: Wait, let me research your Aether Spirit!

t_ch: 你的以太灵好特别啊…只要跟别人对战就能见到各种珍稀生物，以太战线还真是方便。
t_en: Your Aether Spirit is exceptional... Aetherium Wars is so convenient, all I have to do is battle people and I can see all sorts of rare creatures!

t_ch: 可恶，机巧以太灵只是在这个赛季削弱了一点数值，玩家就都说它们是废物…我要亲自证明，机巧不是废物！
t_en: Tsk! Ingenium Aether Spirits only have got nerfed a little this season and players are saying they're trash... I will personally prove that they are not!

t_ch: 果然输了…算了！它们强不强又如何呢？设计者随时都能修改以太灵的能力，但永远修改不了我对它们的喜爱！
t_en: So I did still lose... Forget it! Who cares if they are strong or weak? The designers can rebalance Aether Spirits however they want, they will never change my love for them!

t_ch: 嘿，来场对决吧，随便玩玩就行。
t_en: Hey, let's battle. Just a casual match is fine.

t_ch: 哎呀，输赢都无所谓，玩游戏不就是为了开心嘛。
t_en: Ah, who cares about winning or losing? Gaming is about having fun!

t_ch: 喂，你是新手？让我这个开服元老来给你上一课吧！
t_en: Hey, are you a newbie? I've been playing since the beginning, I'll show you a thing or two!

t_ch: 唉，我居然连新手都打不过了，好怀念刚开服的时候，虽然游戏里只有几个以太灵，还有一堆漏洞，但肯定比现在有趣…曾经的快乐已经一去不返了，你这种新手是不会懂的……
t_en: Ahh, even a noob beat me! I miss when the server had just opened... There may have only been a few Aether Spirits in the game and loads of bugs, but it was way more fun than it is now! I'll never get the joy of those days back, noobs nowadays just won't get it...

t_ch: 哼哼，我托人买来了极品以太灵的数据，就拿你试试威力如何吧！
t_en: Hehe, I got someone to buy me premium Aether Spirit data. I'll test out their strength on you!

t_ch: 可恶，都怪这游戏充钱不能变得更强，不然我才不会输给你！
t_en: Curses! If this game was pay-to-win, I'd never lose to you!

t_ch: 诶，客人，要买本以太战线的攻略吗？至于这攻略靠不靠谱…咱们来场对战就知道了！
t_en: Hey there, buddy! Wanna buy some guidebooks for the Aetherium Wars? As for whether or not it's reliable... How about we have a showdown to find out?

t_ch: 哎哟，居然输了，难道这是上个版本的攻略？客人，不好意思啊，等我进了新货再来吧。
t_en: Ah, I can't believe I lost! Is this a strategy guidebook for the last update? My apologies, dear customer, please find me again when I've updated my stock.

t_ch: 我已经分析过了这款游戏的所有数据，制定出了胜率最高的战略，你是必败无疑的！
t_en: I have already analyzed all of the data in this game and created the strategy with the highest win percentage. Your defeat is inevitable!

t_ch: 看来光分析数据是不够的，还得经过实战检验啊……
t_en: It seems analyzing data isn't enough, I also need real battle experience...

t_ch: 以太战线真是太好玩了，我已经熬夜玩了三天了…跟我来场对决吧，我赢一把就睡……
t_en: Aetherium Wars is so much fun, I've already been playing for three days straight... Battle me! If I win, I'll get some sleep...

t_ch: 哈——哈欠…看来还不能睡，等会儿再打一把，赢一把就睡……
t_en: Yaaawn... Looks like I can't sleep yet. Battle me again! If I win, I'll get some sleep...

t_ch: 科学的力量好厉害！不需要任何电子终端，只要拿着以太硬币就能开始对战！
t_en: The power of science is amazing! We don't need any electronic terminals, we just need to hold our Aether Coins to start a battle!

t_ch: 我居然输了，一定是游戏机制不够科学……
t_en: I can't believe I lost. The game mechanisms can't be true to real science...

t_ch: 你要倒霉了，我手里的可是最强大的以太灵，见识下虚卒们的厉害吧！
t_en: You're out of luck! I have the strongest Aether Spirits around. I'll show you the power of my Voidrangers!

t_ch: 可恶，从原型设定上来说，虚卒可是反物质军团的战士，怎么会打不过其他以太灵呢？游戏的制作组也太不严谨了！
t_en: Blast! Looking at the prototype settings, Voidrangers are Antimatter Legion warriors, how can they not defeat other Aether Spirits? The game devs are a mess!

t_ch: 请留步，这位朋友，玩了这么久的以太战线，你难道就没有感到过一丝反感吗？这些以太灵虽然只是数据，但谁又能否定数据的权益呢？
t_en: Hold it right there. My friend, having played Aetherium Wars for all this time, do you not feel a little disgust? Aethers may only be data, but can we really deny data their own rights?

t_ch: 命令以太灵互相厮杀，让玩家从中取乐，真是太残忍了…如果你还没有感觉，就跟我来一场对决吧，在战斗中好好感受一下以太灵的痛苦！
t_en: Ordering Aether Spirits to massacre each other for the entertainment of players, it's just too cruel... If you have not felt it yet, come and battle with me. I will show you the true suffering of the Aether Spirits!

t_ch: 能有这样的实力，一定是伤害了不少弱小的以太灵吧，希望你能好好思考我的话…不多说了，还有许多玩家需要我用战斗去启迪呢。
t_en: To have power like yours, you must have harmed many weak Aether Spirits. I hope that you will reflect on what I have said... I shall say no more, there are many more players that need me to enlighten them in battle.

t_ch: 总算遇到玩家了，快快快，打完这把我就得去写作业了！
t_en: I finally found a player! Quick, quick! I have to go and do my homework as soon as we're done!

t_ch: 每天就这么点时间能玩游戏，居然还输了，呜呜呜……
t_en: I only get this little time to play every day, and I lost!? Waaaa...

t_ch: 别急着走啊，来看看我的机兵吧！
t_en: Don't rush off, come and look at my automatons!

t_ch: 在现实里我一个机兵都买不起，但在以太战线里我收集了好多机兵，以太战线真是太棒了！
t_en: In the real world, I can't even afford a single automaton. But, in the world of Aetherium Wars, I have collected many! Aetherium Wars is the best!

t_ch: 忙了一天，终于能休息了，来跟我打一场对战吧！
t_en: It's been a long day, finally I get to relax. Come, have a battle with me!

t_ch: 输就输了吧，每天工作这么累，能玩会儿以太战线就是生活中唯一的乐趣了。
t_en: Well, a loss is a loss. I work so hard every day, getting to play Aetherium Wars is my one joy in life.

t_ch: 你听说了吗？只要用特定的以太灵进行九十九次对战，就会刷出隐藏的以太灵！
t_en: Have you heard? If you use specific Aether Spirits in ninety-nine battles, a secret Aether Spirit will spawn!

t_ch: 这已经是第一百次对战了，难道传闻是骗人的……
t_en: That was my hundredth battle... Were the rumors just a lie...?

t_ch: 先别走，呃…我要说什么来着……对了，跟我来一场「以太电线」的对决吧！
t_en: Don't go, uhh... What was I going to say...? Oh, yeah! Let's have an "Eater Room Wards" battle!

t_ch: 哎哟，这游戏的机制也太难了，根本记不住啊……
t_en: Ah, this game is so confusing, I can't remember any of this...

t_ch: 你坐在了你认为正确的马桶上，期待着持有凭证的呜呜伯出现……
t_en: You sit on what you thought was the right toilet, patiently waiting for the Wubbaboo to appear with the pass...

t_ch: ……你在马桶上坐了很久，什么也没有发生，看来你找的不对。
t_en: ...You've been sitting for a long time and nothing has happened. Looks like you're in the wrong place.

t_ch: 突然你感到身下传来一股强大的力量，眼前一片黑。
t_en: Suddenly, you feel a powerful force from below you and your vision goes black.

t_ch: 再次睁开双眼，你发现自己来到了一个陌生的地方，呜呜伯出现在了你的眼前。
t_en: When you once again open your eyes, you realize you've arrived in an unfamiliar place, with the Wubbaboo before you.

t_ch: 启动潜爆机兵
t_en: Activate the Automaton Stealth Bomb

t_ch: 再次睁开双眼，你发现自己来到了一个陌生的地方。
t_en: When you once again open your eyes, you realize you've arrived in an unfamiliar place.

t_ch: 资格徽章已识别
t_en: Mastery Mark identified

t_ch: 潜爆机兵已报废，请重新挑战
t_en: The Automaton Stealth Bomb has been disabled. Please restart the challenge

t_ch: 超出挑战区域，请重新挑战
t_en: You have left the challenge area. Please restart the challenge

t_ch: 你们将潜爆机兵装进了矿车…
t_en: They loaded the Automaton Stealth Bomb into the mine cart...

t_ch: …维尔德？这谁呀？《以太战线》又是啥？
t_en: Huh... Who's Wilder? ...And what's "Aetherium Wars"?

t_ch: 游戏推广，看起来还挺有意思。
t_en: It's an ad for a game. Looks pretty cool.

t_ch: 游戏推广？什么游戏？
t_en: A game ad...?

t_ch: 没什么，又是个推销的罢了。
t_en: It's nothing, just another advertisement.

t_ch: 真的？让我看看……
t_en: Really? Let me see...

t_ch: 唔…利用公司最尖端的以太复印技术，将您见过的任何生物记录在以太硬币中……
t_en: Hmm... using the IPC's cutting-edge Aether copying technology, we can record any creature you've seen inside of an Aether Coin...

t_ch: 再打印出来，与其他玩家切磋对战…让以太灵伙伴为您争取荣誉……
t_en: Just print it out and compete against other players — let your Aether Spirits fight for your glory...

t_ch: 唔…好像还挺有意思的？
t_en: Ooh... Seems pretty neat, don't you think?

t_ch: 是啊是啊，我也觉得。
t_en: I gotta say, sounds pretty cool.

t_ch: 对吧对吧，我早就知道你也是个重度游戏爱好者！咱俩要不顺路去看看？
t_en: Right? I knew you were a gamer! Wanna check it out on the way?

t_ch: 你认真的？
t_en: Seriously?

t_ch: 不然呢，我是真的很感兴趣！咱俩要不顺路去看看？
t_en: I mean... Yeah? Wanna check it out on the way?

t_ch: 走起！
t_en: Let's do it!

t_ch: 太好啦！先前只听说过星核猎手的那位小姑娘有着这套技术——这次说不定能近距离观摩一下。
t_en: Awesome! I've heard that Stellaron Hunter girl uses this kind of tech — maybe we can see it in action.

t_ch: 来都来了…
t_en: Well, since I'm already here...
